message:
perf: use cached PathSpec in _file_reservations_conflict()

Replace raw PathSpec.from_lines() with cached _compile_pathspec()
in the hot conflict-detection path. This was causing O(paths × reservations)
pattern compilations instead of reusing cached specs.

Benchmark: 50 paths × 100 reservations scenario
- Before: 28.89ms (5000 PathSpec compilations)
- After: 2.77ms (cache hits after warmup)
- Improvement: 10.4x

Fixes: mcp_agent_mail-3sd

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -3028,8 +3028,9 @@ def _file_reservations_conflict(existing: FileReservation, candidate_path: str,
     def _normalize(p: str) -> str:
         return p.replace("\\", "/").lstrip("/")
     if PathSpec is not None and GitWildMatchPattern is not None:
-        spec = PathSpec.from_lines("gitwildmatch", [existing.path_pattern])
-        return spec.match_file(_normalize(candidate_path))
+        spec = _compile_pathspec(_normalize_pathspec_pattern(existing.path_pattern))
+        if spec is not None:
+            return spec.match_file(_normalize(candidate_path))
     # Fallback to conservative fnmatch if pathspec not available
     pat = existing.path_pattern
     a = _normalize(candidate_path)
